summary: Shapiro recounts a brief trip to Seoul, South Korea, where he tried and failed to find a traditional woodworking store to buy chisels. He had hoped to find Japanese-style chisels at a lower price or discover distinctly Korean woodworking tools and techniques. Instead, he was repeatedly directed to small power-tool kiosks selling brands like Bosch. He closes by asking readers if anyone knows about Korean woodworking traditions.
key_points: Shapiro traveled to Seoul hoping to buy chisels and explore Korean woodworking traditions
He found no traditional woodworking stores, only modern power-tool kiosks
He crowdsources the question to his blog readers, an early example of using a blog as a community Q&A platform
money_quotes: I might learn something about Korean woodworking and techniques; maybe even pick up a distinctly Korean tool and a few pieces of interesting wood to stash in the carry-on.
I kept getting directed to these tiny power-tool kiosks (I can report that Bosch is popular over there).
key_insight: A lighthearted travel anecdote about the difficulty of finding traditional craft supplies in a modernized city, reflecting Shapiro's early interest in hands-on making.
